issues.body: The new circuit relies on `slot_active = i < counts[g]` to prevent a private antecedent hidden in padding from satisfying membership, but no test places a well-formed antecedent in an inactive slot and proves rejection. Likewise, every test uses K=1, so the strict commitment-order guard and cross-graph attribution accumulation are never exercised. These are security-relevant branches of the claimed committed-membership statement. The module comment also says there is one `should_fail` per rejection clause, which is not true for the ordering clause or inactive-padding case.
issues.fix_hint: Add a `should_fail` test whose rule remains well formed but one required antecedent occurs only at an index >= count, expecting `antecedent not a member of any committed graph`. Add K=2 tests covering a valid split-antecedent derivation with exact `[true, true]` attribution and rejection of non-strict/duplicate commitment ordering (plus incorrect cross-graph attribution if not covered by the valid case). Update the test-suite comment so its coverage claim exactly matches the cases present.
issues.title: Load-bearing padding and multi-graph constraints are not tested

verdict.summary: The relation's rule-shape, commitment recomputation, active-slot membership, and fail-closed host documentation are internally consistent and appropriately caveated as research-grade/not externally audited. However, this soundness-critical circuit lacks tests for two load-bearing constraints that are specifically claimed and implemented: inactive padding must not satisfy antecedent membership, and K>1 commitment ordering/source attribution must work. The test comment's claim of one rejection test per clause is therefore inaccurate, and the untested branches could admit regressions without detection.
